trade.summary: Very strong bilateral trade between two of Europe's most open economies. Netherlands exports chemicals, refined petroleum, agricultural products, machinery, and semiconductor equipment (ASML); Sweden exports vehicles (Volvo, Scania), machinery, pharmaceuticals (AstraZeneca), paper, and wood products. Rotterdam is the primary European port for Swedish imports and exports. Dutch and Swedish multinationals are deeply intertwined through supply chains.

diplomatic.summary: Excellent bilateral relations. Both are liberal democracies with strong multilateralist orientations. Sweden's accession to NATO in 2024 brought Sweden into the same alliance as the Netherlands, further cementing security ties. Both countries are close on EU policy, particularly on trade openness, Ukraine support, and rule-of-law enforcement. Regular high-level consultations on EU and NATO issues.

military.summary: NATO allies since Sweden's 2024 accession. Both countries support Ukraine with military aid. Netherlands and Sweden cooperate on air defense, maritime security in the Baltic and North Seas, and intelligence sharing within NATO frameworks.

financial.summary: Deep bilateral investment. Sweden is a top-10 FDI partner of the Netherlands and vice versa. ASML and Ericsson have major cross-country business ties. Swedish pension funds hold Dutch equities; Dutch pension funds hold Swedish equities. Amsterdam-Stockholm financial corridor is active.

energy.summary: Netherlands supplies Sweden with refined petroleum products through Rotterdam. Both countries are transitioning to renewable energy; cooperation on offshore wind in the North Sea is significant. Sweden exports hydropower-based electricity to Nordic grid neighbors.

scientific.summary: Very active scientific cooperation. ASML-Ericsson supply chain research, Karolinska-AMC medical research collaboration, and extensive EU Horizon Europe joint projects. Both countries are among Europe's top research performers and maintain extensive university exchange programs.
